title: The Encircling Pact, The Warden States, and The Pale Company

the_encircling_pact.founding: Formal Pact document approximately Year 4,000 — though the practical arrangement is older, tracing to the original containment period after the First Reckoning

pact_document.original_version: Kept in a vault in the Inner Zone. Has not been opened in 600 years. The Threshold Keepers know where it is. The Pact's political leadership knows where it is. Nobody has proposed opening it because doing so would require acknowledging what is in it might differ from the current version.
pact_document.core_obligation: Each member state contributes to the containment of the Pale according to its capacity — military, financial, Threshold Keeper provision, or territorial buffer. The specific obligations of each state have been renegotiated in each revision. The original obligations are not known to current members.

current_condition.year_13003: The Encircling Pact is under internal stress that has not been publicly acknowledged. The Still Water States have been underfunding their contribution for 40 years. The Iron Compact is using Pact military resources for external aggression. The Bound Marches is absorbing increasing breach activity without adequate support. The Archive of the Breach in Ashmark has had the full assessment of containment degradation written for three years and has not released it.
current_condition.the_unspoken_crisis: The containment of the Pale is failing. Not rapidly. Not catastrophically — yet. But measurably, and the measurement has been consistent for 80 years. The Threshold Keepers of the Bound Marches know this experientially. The Archive of Ashmark knows it scholastically. The Pale Company knows it operationally. The Pact's political leadership knows it institutionally and has been managing the knowledge rather than the problem.

the_seven_warden_states.name: Valdenmere-Rath
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Hereditary/merit hybrid council — average member age 61, overdue for succession crisis
the_seven_warden_states.character: The oldest continuously governed Warden State. Most orthodox Keeperhood. Most rigorous about Threshold Keeper traditions. Claims unbroken administrative lineage to the original containment period — First Scholars: 60% credible.
the_seven_warden_states.capital: Durnholt — sits exactly at the point where the Inner Zone boundary was first formally marked
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Majority of genuine Threshold Keepers. Institutional memory. Orthodoxy.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: The ruling council's age and the complexity of the succession system. A succession crisis in Valdenmere-Rath would be the worst time for the containment to be failing.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: The most orthodox Keeperhood. Have not forgotten what the faith was originally for.
the_seven_warden_states.player_discovery: Not accessible without significant travel and reputation. A character from this state begins the game knowing about the Pale in specific detail.

the_seven_warden_states.name: The Iron Compact
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Military council with rotating civilian advisory that the commanders treat as advisory
the_seven_warden_states.character: Grew from a military buffer force into a full state over 2,000 years. Adversarial relationship with the Pale — the enemy they have been fighting so long it has become identity.
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Largest standing army of any Warden State. Military response to breach events.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: Using Pact military resources for external territorial aggression. Has been providing the Bound Marches 60% of requested support and calling it adequate. Internal Keeperhood drifting toward Scorched Covenant theology.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: Flame tradition adjacent. Keeperhood chapter drifting toward Scorched Covenant. The Pale is God's enemy and fighting it is worship.
the_seven_warden_states.note: The Ironmark in the Ironmark Borderlands region took its name and organizational model from the Iron Compact — a Hegemony-era breakaway that retained the militant Keeperhood aesthetic without the actual Keeperhood function.

the_seven_warden_states.name: The Still Water States
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Three river valley city-states that function as one for Pact purposes and as three independents for everything else
the_seven_warden_states.character: Richest agricultural land in Cauldenmoor. Feeds significant portions of the Pact. Their relationship with the Pale is the most distant — southern ring, furthest from active breach points.
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Agricultural supply. Food security for the inner states.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: Pact contribution has been declining for 40 years. The threat feels abstract from the southern ring. The other states have not formally raised this yet because doing so would require a Pact council meeting that would also surface the containment degradation assessment.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: Furrow faith dominant. Keeperhood practiced as civic ritual more than genuine vocation.
the_seven_warden_states.the_soilborn_presence: Significant Arnethi Covenant / Soilborn minority in the agricultural communities. Their practical organization against demonic sites is more reliable than the ceremonial Keeperhood in the Still Water States.

the_seven_warden_states.name: Ashmark
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Tripartite: the Archive of the Breach, the Keeperhood chapter, and a hereditary noble council losing ground to the other two
the_seven_warden_states.character: The only Warden State with a genuine academic institution as coequal governance. The Archive of the Breach is the most complete repository of demonic taxonomy, Pale behavior records, and Threshold Keeper documentation in the world.

capital_institution.name: The Archive of the Breach
capital_institution.relationship_to_archive_of_keth: Separate institution. In cautious, irregular communication. Neither shares everything. Both share more than they officially acknowledge.
capital_institution.the_unreleased_assessment: The Archive has had the full assessment of containment degradation written for three years. First loyalty is to accurate information — which means not releasing it until the picture is complete. The picture has been complete for three years. The Archive is deciding how to release it and to whom.

the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Institutional knowledge. Documentation. The long memory.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: First Scholars / Reckoning faith adjacent. Keeperhood here is scholarly rather than devotional.
the_seven_warden_states.player_relevance: The Archive of the Breach is the best in-world resource for Daemon-level knowledge. Access is restricted. A character with appropriate background and reputation can access materials unavailable anywhere else in the world.

the_seven_warden_states.name: The Bound Marches
the_seven_warden_states.ring_position: Directly north of The Pale — most exposed position
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Governing council of Threshold Keeper senior practitioners and elected civilian representatives
the_seven_warden_states.character: The most exposed Warden State. Shares the longest border with the Inner Zone. Threshold Keepers most concentrated here. Absorbs the direct consequences when something comes out. The hardest, most pragmatic, most quietly frightened people in the Pact.
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: The actual work. Everything else in the Pact supports this state's function.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: Threshold Keeper losses elevated for six years. Requesting additional Iron Compact support for three years. Receiving 60% of what was requested. The council has begun discussing what to do if the degradation accelerates before support arrives.

threshold_keepers.description: A hereditary caste that has been managing the Inner Zone boundary for 9,000 years. The most experienced practitioners of anti-demonic craft on Arneth. Their methods are not religious — they are technical, accumulated over millennia of direct engagement.
threshold_keepers.current_status: Under significant strain. Losses are up. The younger generation of Keepers is being put into active service earlier than protocol recommends because there are not enough senior practitioners.
threshold_keepers.the_knowledge_they_hold: The Threshold Keepers know the original Pact obligations — not from the sealed vault, but from continuous practice that preserved the original methods even when the institutional framework around them changed. They know the containment is failing. They know why. They have not been listened to.

the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: The most genuine Keeperhood in the Pact. Not because of theological conviction but because they are reminded regularly of what the faith is actually for.
the_seven_warden_states.pale_company_overlap: A small number of Threshold Keepers and Pale Company Wardens are the same people, or know each other, in ways that neither organization's official record reflects. The overlap is not institutional — it is personal, built over long service in the same territory.

the_seven_warden_states.name: The Solvanic Reach
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Commercial republic — council of major trading interests with a rotating executive
the_seven_warden_states.character: Most recent addition to the Pact — joined approximately Year 12,200 when expansion into Cauldenmoor's interior put it adjacent to the ring. Membership partly genuine commitment, partly commercial logic.
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Financial. Controls main road systems connecting Warden States to Cauldenmoor's broader economy.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: Other states resent the financial-only contribution. Solvanic Reach considers it efficient division of labor. The Keeperhood chapter here is well-funded and politically comfortable — which the Bound Marches' actual Keepers find insulting.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: Tidewarden Church influence significant. Keeperhood chapter well-funded and ceremonial.
the_seven_warden_states.player_relevance: The primary commercial entry point into the Encircling Pact from outside. A character approaching the Pact through trade or commerce will enter through Solvanic Reach. The Pact's relationship with the broader Arneth economy flows through this state.

the_seven_warden_states.name: The Grey States
the_seven_warden_states.ring_position: Northwestern outer ring — borders both Inner Zone and the Grey Between
the_seven_warden_states.governance: Nominally a council of hereditary lords. Actually a maze of internal factions, external arrangements, and unspoken accommodations.
the_seven_warden_states.character: The most politically complicated member of the Pact. Borders both the Inner Zone and the Grey Between — the no-man's-land of Cauldenmoor. History of quiet arrangements with powers outside the Pact. The most interesting place in the Encircling Pact for a player.
the_seven_warden_states.contribution_to_pact: Intelligence. The Grey States' position between the Inner Zone and the Between gives them information unavailable to other members.
the_seven_warden_states.current_stress: The quiet arrangements with outside powers have recently included something that other Pact members are aware of and cannot prove violates the Pact's terms. The Pale Company has more contacts in the Grey States than anywhere else.
the_seven_warden_states.theological_character: Mixed. Keeperhood, Old Ways, Arnethi Covenant, and whispered Compassionate Wound presence coexist. Nobody officially endorses this plurality. Nobody officially stops it.
the_seven_warden_states.pale_company_note: The Grey States produce the majority of known Pale Company contacts — not members, contacts. Whether this is because of their exposure to the Between or because something about their governance produces the specific quality of person the Company looks for is a question the Company's internal records do not resolve.

the_pale.nature: Not a demonic site in the conventional sense. Not a wound that could be healed. A thinness — the boundary between Arneth and whatever is beyond simply does not hold here the way it holds everywhere else. It predates the First Reckoning. It predates the Varathi. It may be the oldest thing on Arneth that is not Arneth.

geography.appearance: The geography is Arneth-standard — hills, scrubland, a river. The light is slightly different. Animals do not enter. Rain that falls here does not drain the same way. At night, people within 20 miles report a specific quality of unease they cannot describe except as awareness — the feeling of being looked at by something without eyes.
geography.the_inner_zone: The 20-mile radius immediately surrounding the Pale. Sparsely populated. The Threshold Keepers and people with nowhere else to go.
geography.from_inside: Accounts disagree. People who go in and come back rarely come back entirely themselves. People who come back intact do not go in twice.

cartography.outside_pact_maps: Blank. No label, no description, absent from the cartography.
what_outsiders_are_told: That's where the cartographer ran out of information
The Warden States don't allow survey of the interior
There's nothing there — just bad land
cartography.player_discovery: The Pale does not appear on any map available to a starting character outside the Warden States. Discovery is a specific game moment — coming over a rise, through a tree line, or along a road that gets quieter the further you go, and then the light is wrong.

the_pale.current_status: The containment is degrading. Documented by the Archive of the Breach for 80 years. Known experientially by the Threshold Keepers of the Bound Marches. Known operationally by the Pale Company. None of these three have formally shared their findings with each other, though there is informal overlap between them.
the_pale.the_varathi_connection: The Varathi may have known about the Pale — there are structural hints in surviving fragments that suggest they built around this region deliberately. The Varathi Collapse may have been partly a failure of whatever the Varathi used to manage it. Scholar-Saint Varath's suppressed texts describe Daemon entities she identified in Varathi records as being connected to the Pale. The Varathi Return cult believes this connection is the key to what they want to restore.

the_pale_company.formal_internal_name: The Order of the Common Soul
the_pale_company.name_origin: The Pale Company takes its name from the Pale — The First Breach. This is not coincidental and not advertised. The Order of the Common Soul is what Wardens call themselves in internal contexts. Almost nobody outside knows this name.
the_pale_company.legend_status: Legendary and spoken of as myth. Most people across Arneth have heard the name. Most do not believe the organization currently exists. The stories are third-hand — a grandfather's account of a stranger who passed through before a problem was resolved, a traveler who seemed to know exactly what was in a ruin without being told. Not dramatic interventions. Quiet ones.

founding.description: No formal founding date. No founding document. The organization emerged from the Warden States' original empirical containment period — the people who went into the Inner Zone and came back, and began to develop the specific quality of perception the Company still selects for.
founding.the_pale_company_and_saint_aldren: The Company's internal records — fragmentary, not complete — reference Saint Aldren of the Marches as a figure who was almost certainly a Company Warden before the Company had a name. His containment methods in the Thornsveld Marches are consistent with Company practice. His burial site may contain the most complete surviving record of early Company methodology.
founding.the_meven_connection: The Company's core identification practice — the way Wardens signal each other in the field — was developed in direct response to Meven's documented identification of compromised individuals. The method Meven used has been encoded, without attribution, into Company core practice. The Company has known about Meven's nature for at least 200 years.

theology.name: Faith in humanity itself
theology.description: Not atheism. Not conventional humanism. Something more specific: the world contains demonstrable supernatural evil. It also contains demonstrable human capacity for both extraordinary good and extraordinary cruelty. The only empirically verified resistance to Daemon influence comes from human beings with sufficient clarity of purpose.
theology.the_creed: What we protect is what is worth protecting. What is worth protecting is the capacity of people to be, sometimes, more than they are. We do not require them to be more than they are. We require only that the capacity remain.
theology.on_humanity: They accept people as they are — the corrupt, the faithless, the criminal — because they understand that moral simplicity is a luxury the Between cannot afford. What they require of their own members is not purity but clarity: the ability to see what something is without the distortion of personal agenda. This is harder than purity and more useful.
theology.on_daemon_nature: The Pale Company is the only organization on Arneth that takes seriously the question of whether Daemon nature is fixed. This does not make them sympathetic to Daemon action. It makes them precise. They do not assume a Daemon is doing what it appears to be doing. They investigate before committing. This has saved lives. It has also, once, led to a catastrophic error in judgment that the organization has never fully discussed openly.

structure.description: Deliberately non-hierarchical in appearance. Actually organized underneath.

members.wardens: Full members. Estimated 40-200 active across all of Arneth at any time. Nobody knows the real number, including probably the Wardens themselves.
members.readers: Not a rank — a function. Wardens who specialize in identification and assessment of demonic sites, Daemon traces, and saint authentication. The most scholarly members.
members.contacts: People who are not Wardens but who have a relationship with the Company — provide information, shelter, resources. Some contacts become Wardens. Most don't. Most don't know they are contacts until they are.

structure.governance: Decisions by consensus of available senior Wardens. Practically, most decisions are made by whoever is present and trusted. No central location. No permanent headquarters.
structure.the_pact_overlap: A small number of Threshold Keepers and Pale Company Wardens are the same people. This is not institutional overlap — it is personal. Built over long service in the same territory.

induction.overview: You don't apply. You don't find them. They find you — or more accurately, they notice you. The process takes months to years. The first contact is coincidental. The second is assessment disguised as assistance. By the third, both parties know what is happening.
what_they_look_for: Demonstrated capacity to see clearly under pressure
No institutional loyalty that overrides judgment — not no loyalties, but no loyalty that has stopped them seeing accurately
Survival in situations that would have ended someone with less practical wisdom
Some quality of presence around demonic influence — not immunity, but specific resistance that is not purely physical courage
The capacity for inaction — knowing when not to act is as important as acting
induction.what_induction_looks_like: Not a ceremony. Not a title. A conversation in which both parties acknowledge what the player has become without quite saying it directly. The new Warden receives one thing: the signal.

the_signal.description: A specific way of doing something ordinary that any Warden will recognize and no one else will notice. Not a physical object. Not a spoken word. A gesture embedded in a common action — the way a cup is set down, the way a door is held, the way a name is said when meeting someone. Wardens recognize it immediately. Non-Wardens see nothing.
the_signal.origin: Derived from Meven's identification method — the way Meven distinguished genuine from corrupted involved a specific quality of attention that has been encoded into the signal. A Daemon acting as human will respond to the signal incorrectly. A human compromised by demonic influence will respond incorrectly. A genuine human, Warden or not, will respond correctly without knowing why.

reputation_mechanics.player_visibility: Hidden. The UI never shows it. No progress bar. No notification. No labeled milestone.
what_increments_it: Actions taken when the player believed they were unobserved
Refusals — contracts not taken, coin not accepted, things not done that would have been profitable
Accurate identification of spiritual wrongness before physical manifestation
Protecting someone with no strategic value to the player
Telling the truth when lying would have been easier and undetectable
Surviving an encounter with demonic presence with judgment intact
The specific quality of not panicking in the presence of something that warrants panic
Inaction — walking away from something profitable because it was wrong; refusing something easy because of what it required
what_does_not_increment_it: Combat effectiveness against demonic entities (this helps but is not the measure)
Acts of obvious heroism that had an audience
Doing the right thing when it was the easy thing
reputation_mechanics.first_contact_threshold: When the counter reaches approximately 40% of maximum, the Warden assigned to this region makes first contact. The contact is deniable. The conversation can be walked away from without consequence.
reputation_mechanics.induction_threshold: No formal threshold. A judgment call by the contacting Warden. The counter is one input. Direct observation is another. A player with a very high counter who presents badly in person may wait longer. A player with a moderate counter who demonstrates something specific in a high-stakes moment may be contacted earlier.
reputation_mechanics.the_hardest_reputation: The Pale Company reputation is the hardest to gain in a knowing matter because actions taken specifically to gain it are weighted less than actions taken without awareness of being observed. A player who figures out the system and tries to farm it will find the counter moving more slowly than a player who simply acts according to their character. This is intentional and correct.

physical_presence.appearance: They carry nothing that marks them. No insignia, no uniform, no weapon type. A Warden looks like whatever background they came from — former soldier, former scholar, former criminal.
physical_presence.the_only_consistent_marker: A quality of attention. The way they look at a room. The way they stand in relation to a door. Visible to other Wardens. Not visible to most others, but occasionally a perceptive person notices something they cannot name.
physical_presence.weapons: Personal, maintained with extraordinary care. Not ritual — practical. A Warden who loses their weapon in the Between may not come back.

relationships_with_factions.relationship: Cautious mutual respect. Have collaborated. Neither fully trusts the other. The Ember Wardens' institutional faith makes the Company uncomfortable. The Company's lack of institutional faith makes the Ember Wardens uncomfortable.

Functional distrust. The Purifiers' methods create community trauma that demonic influence exploits. The Company has cleaned up after Purifier actions twice. The Purifiers consider the Company either a myth or a rival.
The closest institutional friendship the Company has. Both value accurate information over comfortable doctrine. They share intelligence when it serves neither's institutional interest specifically.

The Company knows about Meven. Has known for at least 200 years. Has not disclosed because the Order does genuine good. Currently watching the Order's internal division about disclosure carefully. One senior Warden believes disclosure may be approaching — the containment degradation makes the full truth about Daemon nature operationally relevant.

Possibly the most interesting relationship. Both organizations are information networks that appear smaller than they are. Both operate through observation rather than force where possible. Whether they overlap in membership is unknown and probably the point. Neither has formally acknowledged the other.

The most significant philosophical overlap of any faith. Both non-hierarchical. Both trust demonstrated action over stated belief. Both treat demonic presence as a practical problem rather than theological abstraction. More Pale Company Wardens came from Soilborn backgrounds than from any other faith.

The political leadership considers the Company a historical relic with no current relevance. The Threshold Keepers of the Bound Marches have a different view. The Company's internal assessment: the political leadership's management of the containment crisis rather than addressing it is the current most dangerous situation on Arneth.

the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.identity: Soven — the Barefoot Compact walker who has been passing through Durnward for three months
the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.background: Was a genuine Barefoot Compact walker before he became a Warden. The two are not incompatible — the Compact's non-institutional wandering life produces the specific quality of observation the Company selects for.
the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.why_he_is_here: Something in the eastern ridgeline near the Durnwald Mine is active. Not newly active — reactivated. Saint Aldren's historical containment there is degrading. The mine collapse may have been natural. The reason that section of the mine degraded to the point of collapse may not have been.
the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.what_he_knows: The Saint Aldren containment markers — the discs. The degradation timeline. The connection between the ridgeline site and the broader pattern of containment failure that the Company has been tracking.
the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.what_he_has_not_done: Made contact with anyone he trusts enough to work with. Has been watching Morra Dawnfield for two weeks. Has been watching Torven Weld for three weeks. Has been watching the player-character since they arrived.
the_warden_in_thornsveld_marches.first_contact: Will feel like a conversation about nothing in particular — he will say something that could be coincidence or could be a test. It happens in the common room of the Marchwarden's Rest, in the evening, on a quiet night when most regulars have gone. He sits down without asking. He says: 'You've been here a while.' Then he waits.

arnethi_covenant.name: The Arnethi Covenant
arnethi_covenant.name_origin: Soilborn is a slight from other faiths that they adopted as a self-descriptor
arnethi_covenant.core_theology: Arneth is Heaven. The physical world is sacred — land, water, living things, human bodies. Demons are not God's punishment or test. They are the assault of Hell on Heaven. Resistance is not just survival — it is devotion.
arnethi_covenant.on_the_angel_absence: Not a theological problem for the Soilborn. Heaven does not send angels to defend itself — Heaven is defended by the people who live in it. The silence is not absence. It is the sound of people doing the work.
arnethi_covenant.on_death: Not a passage to a better place. A loss of something sacred. Their grief rites are the most intense of any faith on Arneth. There is no comfort of afterlife to offer — only the truth that what was lost was real and the loss is real.
arnethi_covenant.on_suffering: The ongoing assault of Hell on Heaven. Not divine punishment. Not test. Assault. This makes the Soilborn simultaneously the most hopeful faith on Arneth (we are in the right place) and the most urgently mobilized (it is under attack).
arnethi_covenant.institutional_character: Decentralized. No cathedral hierarchy. No political structure above regional level. Organized around Groundkeepers — lay clergy who tend both the land and the congregation. Authority from demonstrated competence at both.
arnethi_covenant.community_response_to_demonic: The most immediate and practically organized of any faith. No militant wing — the whole community is the militant wing. Effective against lesser demonic and demonic site activity. Less effective against Daemon-level events because their scale of organization does not match the scale of the threat.
arnethi_covenant.geographic_presence: Strongest in agricultural communities, particularly Cauldenmoor's temperate middle. Significant minority in the Free Fens — overlap with Moor Communion in land-reverence makes mixed practice common.
arnethi_covenant.relationship_with_pale_company: Significant philosophical overlap. More Wardens came from Soilborn backgrounds than any other faith.
arnethi_covenant.political_vulnerability: No institutional power structure means no political leverage. The Soilborn have been periodically suppressed by powers that found their theology threatening — specifically, the implication that Arneth as Heaven means its desecration is a religious offense, which creates conflict with lords who desecrate land for profit.

order_of_the_compassionate_wound.name: The Order of the Compassionate Wound
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.name_meaning: The wound that remains open because closing it would stop the bleeding but also stop the feeling. Compassionate because it is chosen.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.origin: Emerged from the Warden States' Buffer Zone approximately Year 11,800 during a period of containment weakness and demonic overflow. Founded around Meven's work and methods.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.geographic_presence: Primarily Cauldenmoor's interior and selected border regions. Has spread outward from the Warden States over centuries.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.public_character: Hospice care. Feeding the destitute. Tending the dying regardless of faith affiliation. The reputation is real and earned. The people who run local chapters are genuinely good by most measures.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.the_secret: Meven was a Daemon. The Order has known for 400 years. The evidence has been suppressed three times.

current_internal_division.faction_keep_secret: The secret must be kept forever. The truth would destroy an institution that does genuine good. The dead do not need to be re-examined. The work continues.
current_internal_division.faction_reveal: Building a foundation of compassion on a lie is a kind of violence. The secret must be released because the truth matters more than the institution's comfort.
current_internal_division.faction_liberation: The truth is liberating. Proof that the divide between Daemon and other is not absolute. Meven chose forty years of genuine good. This changes everything. It should be used.

order_of_the_compassionate_wound.recovery_practice: The Order has the best documented recovery practices for incubus/succubus influence of any institution on Arneth. These practices trace to Meven. The Order does not advertise this capability — doing so would invite the question of where the practices came from.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.relationship_with_keeperhood: Complicated. The Order emerged inside the Warden States. The Keeperhood knows about Meven. Neither speaks of this in formal contexts.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.relationship_with_pale_company: The Company knows. Has not disclosed. Is watching. One senior Warden believes disclosure time is approaching — the containment degradation makes Daemon nature questions operationally urgent.
order_of_the_compassionate_wound.burial_site_of_meven: Within Warden States territory. The second strongest anomalous resistance on Arneth after Saint Aldric's burial site. The Order guards it. The Varathi Return cult has been trying to access it for 60 years. The Keeperhood has institutional interest in a saint site within their territory. The political structure of the Encircling Pact has an opinion about who actually controls it.
